g.data-narration: Welcome to this lecture on Multiple Inheritance in C++. We'll explore this powerful OOP feature thoroughly.
Multiple inheritance allows a derived class to inherit from two or more base classes, combining properties from different hierarchies.
g.text: Enables a class to combine functionalities from different domains

g.data-narration: Consider a Teaching Assistant - they need properties from both Faculty (teaching) and Student (enrollment) classes.
The syntax uses a comma-separated list of base classes with optional access specifiers for each.
Base constructors execute in declaration order, not initializer list order. Destructors run in reverse.
g.text: Order follows declaration, not initializer list!

g.data-narration: When two base classes have members with the same name, ambiguity arises. The compiler cannot decide which to use.
Use the scope resolution operator (::) to explicitly specify which base class member to access.
The Diamond Problem occurs when a class inherits from two classes that share a common base, creating duplicate subobjects.
g.text: A class inherits from two classes that share a common ancestor.

g.data-narration: The diamond-shaped inheritance hierarchy shows how MostDerived gets two paths to access the common base class.
Virtual inheritance uses the virtual keyword to ensure only ONE instance of the common base class exists.
With virtual inheritance, only ONE shared Person subobject exists. MostDerived must initialize the virtual base.
With virtual inheritance: virtual bases first, then direct bases in declaration order, then most derived class.
Let's see a complete example with Employee and Manager base classes, and TeamLeader as the derived class.
This example demonstrates ambiguity resolution using the scope resolution operator to access members from specific base classes.
The classic diamond problem example shows virtual inheritance solving the duplicate Person subobject issue.
g.text: Example 3: Diamond Problem with Virtual Inheritance

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: Multiple inheritance syntax, ambiguity resolution with ::, diamond problem solved by virtual inheritance.
g.text: CSE - OOP Unit 3 | Practice multiple inheritance to master this concept!
